info.title: calor
info.description: Calor compiler CLI - Convert C# to Calor and compile Calor to C#

x-inspectra.cliParsedDescription: Calor Compiler - Compiles Calor source to C# and migrates between languages

options.name: --input
options.description: The Calor source file to compile

options.name: --output
options.description: The output C# file path

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Enable verbose output

options.name: --strict-api
options.description: Enable strict API mode: requires §BREAKING markers for public API changes

options.name: --require-docs
options.description: Require documentation on public functions and types

options.name: --enforce-effects
options.description: Enforce effect declarations (default: false; opt in for strict checking)

options.name: --strict-effects
options.description: Promote unknown external call warnings (Calor0411) to errors

options.name: --permissive-effects
options.description: Permissive effect mode: unknown calls assumed pure, forbidden effects demoted to warnings. Designed for converted code.

options.name: --contract-mode
options.description: Contract enforcement mode: off, debug, or release (default: debug)

options.name: --verify
options.description: Enable static contract verification with Z3 SMT solver

options.name: --no-cache
options.description: Disable verification result caching

options.name: --clear-cache
options.description: Clear verification cache before compiling

options.name: --verification-timeout
options.description: Z3 solver timeout per contract in milliseconds (default: 5000)

options.name: --no-telemetry
options.description: Disable anonymous usage telemetry

options.name: --analyze
options.description: Enable advanced verification analyses (dataflow, bug patterns, taint tracking)

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information

options.name: -h
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: convert
commands.description: Convert a single file between C# and Calor

options.name: --output
options.description: The output file path (auto-detected if not specified)

options.name: --benchmark
options.description: Include benchmark metrics comparison

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Enable verbose output

options.name: --explain
options.description: Show detailed explanation of unsupported features

options.name: --no-fallback
options.description: Fail conversion when encountering unsupported constructs (instead of emitting TODO comments)

options.name: --validate
options.description: Parse the generated Calor output and report any errors before writing

options.name: --timeout
options.description: Timeout in seconds for the conversion (0 = no timeout)

arguments.name: INPUT
arguments.description: The source file to convert (.cs or .calr)

commands.name: migrate
commands.description: Migrate an entire project between C# and Calor

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Preview changes without writing files

options.name: --benchmark
options.description: Include before/after metrics

options.name: --direction
options.description: Migration direction (cs-to-calor or calor-to-cs)

options.name: --parallel
options.description: Run conversions in parallel

options.name: --report
options.description: Save migration report to file (supports .md or .json)

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Enable verbose output

options.name: --skip-analyze
options.description: Skip the migration analysis phase

options.name: --skip-verify
options.description: Skip the Z3 contract verification phase

options.name: --verification-timeout
options.description: Z3 verification timeout per contract in milliseconds

arguments.name: PATH
arguments.description: The project directory or .csproj file to migrate

commands.name: benchmark
commands.description: Compare Calor vs C# across 7 evaluation categories

options.name: --calor
options.description: The Calor file to benchmark

options.name: --csharp
options.description: The C# file to benchmark

options.name: --category
options.description: Filter by category (TokenEconomics, GenerationAccuracy, Comprehension, EditPrecision, ErrorDetection, InformationDensity, TaskCompletion)

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format (console, markdown, json)

options.name: --output
options.description: Save benchmark results to file

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Show detailed per-metric breakdown

options.name: --quick
options.description: Use quick token-only benchmark (skip full 7-metric evaluation)

arguments.name: PROJECT
arguments.description: The project directory to benchmark (optional)

commands.name: init
commands.description: Initialize the current directory for Calor development with AI coding agents

options.name: --ai
options.description: AI agent(s) to configure (can specify multiple): claude, codex, gemini, github

options.name: --project
options.description: The .csproj file to configure (auto-detects if single .csproj exists)

options.name: --solution
options.description: The .sln or .slnx file to configure (initializes all projects in solution)

options.name: --force
options.description: Overwrite existing files without prompting

commands.name: format
commands.description: Format Calor source files to canonical style

options.name: --check
options.description: Check if files are formatted without modifying them (exit 1 if not formatted)

options.name: --write
options.description: Write formatted output back to the file(s)

options.name: --diff
options.description: Show diff of formatting changes

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Enable verbose output

arguments.name: FILES
arguments.description: The Calor source file(s) to format

commands.name: lint
commands.description: Check and fix Calor code for agent-optimal format

options.name: --fix
options.description: Auto-fix lint issues by reformatting

options.name: --check
options.description: Check only, exit 1 if issues found

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Show detailed lint issues

arguments.name: FILES
arguments.description: The Calor source file(s) to lint

commands.name: diagnose
commands.description: Output machine-readable diagnostics for Calor files

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format: text, json, or sarif

options.name: --output
options.description: Output file (stdout if not specified)

options.name: --strict-api
options.description: Enable strict API checking

options.name: --require-docs
options.description: Require documentation on public functions

options.name: --validate-codegen
options.description: Validate generated C# code for syntax errors

options.name: --permissive-effects
options.description: Use permissive effect enforcement: unknown calls assumed pure, forbidden-effect checks demoted to warnings

arguments.name: FILES
arguments.description: The Calor source file(s) to diagnose

commands.name: assess
commands.description: Assess C# files for Calor migration potential

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format: text, json, or sarif

options.name: --output
options.description: Output file (stdout if not specified)

options.name: --threshold
options.description: Minimum score to include (0-100)

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Show detailed per-file breakdown

options.name: --top
options.description: Number of top files to show

arguments.name: PATH
arguments.description: Directory to assess recursively

commands.name: analyze-convertibility
commands.description: Analyze how likely C# code is to successfully convert to Calor

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format: text or json

options.name: --output
options.description: Output file (stdout if not specified)

options.name: --quick
options.description: Stage 1 only (no conversion attempt)

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Show per-file breakdown for directories

arguments.name: PATH
arguments.description: File or directory to analyze

commands.name: hook
commands.description: AI agent hook commands for Calor-first enforcement

commands.name: validate-write
commands.description: Validate a Write tool call to enforce Calor-first development

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format: 'gemini' for JSON response, default for exit codes only

arguments.name: TOOL_INPUT
arguments.description: The JSON tool input from the AI agent

commands.name: validate-edit
commands.description: Validate an Edit tool call to enforce Calor-first development

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format: 'gemini' for JSON response, default for exit codes only

arguments.name: TOOL_INPUT
arguments.description: The JSON tool input from the AI agent

commands.name: validate-calr-content
commands.description: Check .calr file content for semantic version declaration

arguments.name: TOOL_INPUT
arguments.description: The JSON tool input from the AI agent

commands.name: post-write-lint
commands.description: Run lint check after .calr file is written

arguments.name: TOOL_INPUT
arguments.description: The JSON tool input from the AI agent

commands.name: validate-ids
commands.description: Validate IDs in .calr file content

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format: 'gemini' for JSON response, default for exit codes only

arguments.name: TOOL_INPUT
arguments.description: The JSON tool input from the AI agent

commands.name: ids
commands.description: Manage Calor declaration IDs

commands.name: check
commands.description: Validate IDs (missing, duplicates, invalid format)

options.name: --allow-test-ids
options.description: Allow test IDs (f001, m001) in all locations

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Show detailed output

arguments.name: PATHS
arguments.description: Paths to files or directories to check

commands.name: assign
commands.description: Add missing IDs to declarations

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Show changes without modifying files

options.name: --fix-duplicates
options.description: Reassign duplicate IDs (keeps first occurrence)

options.name: --allow-test-ids
options.description: Allow test IDs (f001, m001) - skip those without assigning

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Show detailed output

arguments.name: PATHS
arguments.description: Paths to files or directories to process

commands.name: index
commands.description: Generate an index of all IDs in the project

options.name: --output
options.description: Output file path (default: calor.ids.json)

arguments.name: PATHS
arguments.description: Paths to files or directories to index

commands.name: effects
commands.description: Work with effect declarations and manifests

commands.name: resolve
commands.description: Resolve effects for a method signature

options.name: --project
options.description: Project directory for loading project-local manifests

options.name: --solution
options.description: Solution directory for loading solution-level manifests

options.name: --json
options.description: Output in JSON format

arguments.name: SIGNATURE
arguments.description: Method signature to resolve (e.g., 'System.Console.WriteLine' or 'File.ReadAllText')

commands.name: validate
commands.description: Validate all manifests in search path

options.name: --project
options.description: Project directory for loading project-local manifests

options.name: --solution
options.description: Solution directory for loading solution-level manifests

commands.name: list
commands.description: List all types with effect declarations

options.name: --project
options.description: Project directory for loading project-local manifests

options.name: --solution
options.description: Solution directory for loading solution-level manifests

options.name: --type
options.description: Filter by type name (partial match)

options.name: --json
options.description: Output in JSON format

commands.name: verify
commands.description: Verify contracts in Calor files using Z3 SMT solver

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format: text or json

options.name: --output
options.description: Output file (stdout if not specified)

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Enable verbose output with detailed verification info

options.name: --timeout
options.description: Z3 solver timeout per contract in milliseconds (default: 5000)

options.name: --no-cache
options.description: Disable verification result caching

options.name: --clear-cache
options.description: Clear verification cache before verifying

arguments.name: FILES
arguments.description: The Calor source file(s) to verify

commands.name: lsp
commands.description: Start the Calor Language Server (stdio transport)

commands.name: mcp
commands.description: Start the Calor MCP server for AI coding agents

options.name: --stdio
options.description: Use standard input/output for communication (default: true)

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Enable verbose output to stderr for debugging

commands.name: feature-check
commands.description: Check if a C# feature is supported in Calor conversion

options.name: --list
options.description: List all known features and their support levels

options.name: --level
options.description: Filter by support level: full, partial, notsupported, manualrequired

arguments.name: FEATURE
arguments.description: The C# feature to check (e.g., 'yield-return', 'async-await', 'linq-method')

commands.name: coverage
commands.description: Analyze a C# file for Calor conversion coverage and blockers

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Include detailed dimension scores and examples

arguments.name: FILE
arguments.description: The C# file to analyze for Calor conversion coverage

commands.name: self-test
commands.description: Verify compiler correctness by compiling embedded reference files and diffing output against golden files

options.name: --scenario
options.description: Run only a specific scenario by name (e.g., '01_hello_world')

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Show detailed output including diffs for failures
